title: An Improvisation
content:
The stars cleave the sky.
        Yet for us they rest,
    And their race-course high
        Is a shining nest!

    The hours hurry on.
        But where is thy flight,
    Soft pavilion
        Of motionless night?

    Earth gives up her trees
        To the holy air;
    They live in the breeze;
        They are saints at prayer!

    Summer night, come from God,
        On your beauty, I see,
    A still wave has flowed
        Of eternity!
meaning:
This evocative piece by George MacDonald, titled "An Improvisation", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
